feedback.Feedback: Build a trackable project board. Every completed phase or small goal must be recorded. Every feedback and modification must leave records for rollback.

"Project board items disappeared from the dashboard" — `dashboard_data.json` showed `goal: ""` and 0 items for every project because `build_dashboard_v2.py` regex required `**bold**` `Goal` / `Exit criteria` / `Initial tasks` / `Evidence` headings but the project boards used plain headings.

changes.Change: Created the initial project tracking board and documentation structure.
changes.Rollback: Revert initial project-board files or restore from the first git checkpoint

changes.Change: Initialized local git versioning and recorded the first rollback checkpoint.
changes.Rollback: Revert the checkpoint-record update commit; keep or remove `.git` depending on whether version history is still needed

changes.Change: Fixed the `Goal` / `Exit criteria` / `Initial tasks` / `Evidence` regex in `build_dashboard_v2.py` so it matches plain (non-`**bold**`) project-board headings, and made all four regexes accept either bold or plain form via `(?: \*\*)?` while tolerating leading indent on bullets. This is why `dashboard_data.json` had `goal: ""` and zero items for every project in v1. Added 4 entries / 9 sections / 6 phases back into the dashboard. Also added `lang="zh-CN"` + EN ↔ 中 toggle button + `data-i18n` attribute scaffolding (`pageTitle`, `appName`, `tagline`, `generated` prefix) in v2.
changes.Files/Areas Affected: `build_dashboard_v2.py` (lines ~85-138), `dashboard.html` (head + header + `templates/i18n.js` not yet wired), `dashboard_data.json` (rebuilt with full content), new file `build_dashboard_v2.py.sv` saved before v1 and v2 writes
changes.Rollback: Restore `.bak` and `.sv` files, run `python3 build_dashboard.py` instead of v2 to confirm v1 produces the old (broken) shape, or revert commit

rollback_levels.title: Content correction
rollback_levels.body:
Use when only wording, task status, or metadata is wrong.

Action:
- Edit the affected Markdown entry.
- Add a new `CHG-*` entry explaining the correction.

rollback_levels.title: Single change rollback
rollback_levels.body:
Use when one implementation or content change should be undone.

Action:
- Use `git revert <commit-hash>` if the change was committed.
- Update `ROLLBACK.md` with the rollback result.
- Add a `LOGBOOK.md` entry.

rollback_levels.title: Phase rollback
rollback_levels.body:
Use when a whole phase direction is rejected.

Action:
- Identify all `CHG-*` entries in that phase.
- Revert their commits in reverse chronological order.
- Move affected kanban tasks back to `Backlog` or `Next`.
- Add a decision entry explaining why the phase was rolled back.
